name: Chambers Bay
address: 6320 Grandview Drive West, University Place, WA 98467
green_fee_notes: 2026 dynamic starting rates run $109-$209 for Pierce County residents and $149-$325 for non-residents depending on month, with tax-inclusive advance-booking rates of $300-$425 from April through October.
notable: Hosted the 2015 U.S. Open (won by Jordan Spieth), the first U.S. Open in the Pacific Northwest
Walking-only links course built on a reclaimed sand-and-gravel mine on Puget Sound
Owned by Pierce County and operated by KemperSports; also hosted the 2010 U.S. Amateur and 2022 U.S. Women's Amateur
Future USGA host: 2027 U.S. Junior Amateur and 2033 U.S. Amateur

confidence.tees_ratings: All ratings/slopes cross-verified identical between the official 10/2025 scorecard PDF and USGA NCRDB (CourseID 1456). The USGA-only Champ tee (7,867 yds, 77.6/145) and the combo tee sets are omitted from the directory card.
confidence.green_fee_notes: From the 2026 rate table on chambersbaygolf.com as of July 2026; figures are dynamic starting prices.
confidence.lat_lng: Centroid of the OSM course polygon (way 26787026).
